texto: Hatred, like a bush fire, ultimately consumes those who propagate it, leaving nothing but scorched, barren earth behind in their hearts. Love, the greatest of reckless endeavours, inspires men to greatness in the face of seemingly insurmountable odds... Maybe this book is just that, a reckless endeavour of the heart.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stephen Lee: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Hatred, like a bush fire, ultimately consumes those who propagate it, leaving nothing but scorche…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, book, books pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e termino.
